content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory architectures can be reengineered to weight memory retention by the intrinsic prediction error generated by an ICM curiosity mechanism, creating a "curiosity-aligned memory cache" that only preserves high-uncertainty, exploration-driving experiences over long agent lifetimes. This avoids the common problem of catastrophic unlearning of useful exploratory patterns and eliminates memory bloat from trivial, low-surprise observations.
